title: Acetaminophen and Codeine: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
s for this medicine 
This medication may be prescribed for other uses; ask your doctor or pharmacist for more information.
What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking acetaminophen and codeine, tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are allergic to this drug, any part of this drug, or any other 
drugs, foods or substances. Tell your doctor or pharmacist about the allergy and what 
symptoms you had. some medications should not be taken with acetaminophen and codeine. Make sure you have discussed any 
medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting acetaminophen and codeine with your doctor 
and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking acetaminophen and codeine, 
please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. Tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking the following medications or have stopped taking them within the past two weeks: isocarboxazid, linezolid, methylene blue, phenelzine, selegiline, or tranylcypromine. the following nonprescription products may interact with acetaminophen and codeine: St. John's wort and tryptophan. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking acetaminophen and codeine.
